title: 10 best herbal teas for eye twitching

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with eye twitching because of its ability to promote relaxation and calmness in the body. The tea's sedative properties work to soothe the nervous system, which can become overactive and lead to muscle spasms, such as those that cause eye twitching. By calming the nerves, valerian tea helps to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of balance and tranquility, thereby alleviating the spasms and twitching. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to find relief from the discomfort and annoyance caused by eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian tea's sedative properties can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and spasms that may cause eye twitching.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to eye twitching symptoms.

Muscle relaxant and spasmolytic effects: Valerian tea has been shown to relax muscle spasms and cramps, including those that affect the muscles around the eyes, which can help alleviate eye twitching symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which has been shown to help relax muscles and calm nervous system activity, reducing eye twitching symptoms.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a unique blend of essential oils and compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, providing relief from eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried valerian root from a reputable source weighing approximately one tablespoon.
Boil filtered water in a medium saucepan and reduce heat to a gentle simmer for ten minutes.
Add the dried valerian root to the boiling water and let it steep for twelve to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea leaves through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool slightly before consuming immediately as it is best served warm within one hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with eye twitching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The flavonoids present in the herb have been shown to reduce inflammation and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of eye twitching. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, maypop tea can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension that lead to twitching. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the herb may also help to reduce redness and puffiness in the eyes, providing relief from irritated or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop tea's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye twitching and other vision-related issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in maypop tea can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which may be contributing to eye twitching and discomfort.
Muscle relaxant properties: Maypop tea's muscle-relaxing compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help calm and relax the tiny muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye twitching.
Buds: The buds contain bioactive compounds like salicylic acid, which is known for its ability to soothe irritated eyes and reduce twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried maypop flowers and leaves in amounts of 1 tablespoon per cup.
Combine harvested herbs with boiling water in a teapot for steeping time of 5-7 minutes.
Strain tea mixture using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup for serving.
Drink the resulting tea immediately, dosed at 1-2 cups daily for optimal results.
Refrigerate any leftover tea and consume within 24 hours to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with eye twitching because of its calming and soothing properties. The flavonoids present in lavender, such as luteolin and apigenin, have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often underlying causes of eye twitching. The tea's gentle, relaxing effect on the nervous system also helps to calm the muscles around the eyes, providing relief from spasms and twitches. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may help reduce inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lavender oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, may help reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, thereby alleviating eye twitching.

Chamazulene's antispasmodic properties: Chamazulene, a sesquiterpene lactone found in lavender, has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help calm down spasms and contractions in the muscles around the eyes that may be contributing to eye twitching.

Lavender's GABA-enhancing properties: Lavender oil contains compounds that can enhance the activity of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle tone and relaxation. This can lead to a reduction in muscle spasms and twitching around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and relax muscles around the eyes.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm eye twitching caused by stress or allergies.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried english lavender flowers and measure out one teaspoon for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the measured water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the dried lavender flowers to the boiling water and let steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly, savoring its calming effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with eye twitching because its unique blend of compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, has a calming effect on the nervous system. These natural chemicals help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye twitching. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate any underlying inflammation or irritation that could be contributing to the twitching. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, maidenhair tree teas can provide relief from frequent and annoying eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Adenosine antagonism: Maidenhair tea's adenosine-blocking compounds help to reduce the contraction of small blood vessels around the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in maidenhair tea have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate inflammation-related eye twitching symptoms.
Muscle relaxant properties: Maidenhair tea contains compounds like scopoletin, which has a muscle-relaxing effect on the eyes' extraocular muscles, helping to reduce involuntary spasms and twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks contain astringent properties that help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured ginkgo biloba leaves with the boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and reserve the liquid.
Pour the tea into a cup and consume immediately, ideally on an empty stomach with no additives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with eye twitching because of its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, common underlying causes of eye spasms. The herbal tea's antidepressant properties help soothe the nervous system, calming the muscles around the eyes and alleviating involuntary twitches. Additionally, St John's Wort's anti-inflammatory effects can also help relieve any underlying irritation or strain that may be contributing to the twitching. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, this natural remedy provides a holistic approach to addressing eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate eye twitching caused by inflammation or irritation of the muscles around the eyes.
Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort is known for its adaptogenic effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-induced muscle spasms, including eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making it effective for soothing eye twitches.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm irritated eyes and reduce twitching.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum at least 4 teaspoons.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried st john's wort flowers and place it into a tea infuser or muslin bag.
Bring filtered water to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer for 5 minutes allowing flavors to steep.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup discarding solids after 10-15 minutes of steeping time.
Enjoy your st john's wort tea while warm and consume twice daily for optimal eye twitch relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with eye twitching because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation and relax the tiny muscles around the eyes, calming spasms and twitches. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects on the nervous system help to calm stress and anxiety, common triggers for eye twitching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, littleleaf linden teas offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating eye twitching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Littleleaf Linden tea's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles around the eyes and reduce spasms or contractions that may be contributing to eye twitching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Littleleaf Linden tea may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching and other eye discomforts.

Calming and sedative properties: Littleleaf Linden tea's calming and sedative properties can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to calm and soothe eye muscles, reducing twitching and spasms.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, alleviating twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves per cup water.
Heat filtered water to a boil and steep the leaves for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before consuming within three hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with eye twitching because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The plant's natural compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce inflammation and tension in the eyes and surrounding muscles, effectively alleviating spasms and twitches. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the twitching. As a result, regular consumption of yarrow tea can provide lasting relief from eye twitching and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with eye twitching.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in yarrow tea can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching caused by stress, anxiety, or other factors.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tea's antioxidants can help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe and calm eye twitching caused by inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce eye stress and promote relaxation of the eyelid muscles to alleviate twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of fresh or dried yarrow flowers achillea millefolium leaves and stems.
Combine harvested herbs with one cup boiling water for five minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool for ten to fifteen minutes before consumption serving warm.
Consume one cup of the yarrow herbal tea three times daily for at least two weeks treatment duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with eye twitching because of its natural calming properties. The tea's active compound, rosmarinic acid, has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the tiny muscles around the eyes that are responsible for twitching. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation. By sipping on this calming tea, individuals can enjoy a sense of tranquility and find relief from eye twitching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to muscle spasms, including eye twitching.
Muscle relaxant properties: The volatile oils in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on muscles, which can help relieve tension and spasms causing eye twitching.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's ability to reduce anxiety and stress levels can help alleviate the physical symptoms of eye twitching, including muscle contractions and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm are used in tea to calm nerves and reduce stress, which can contribute to eye twitching.
Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm contain calming compounds that help soothe eye twitching by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried or one tablespoon of fresh lemon balm leaves per cup.
Combine the measured lemon balm with boiling water in a teapot or infuser for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste if desired after cooling slightly.
Drink the tea immediately and re-brew as needed up to three times throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap teas helps with eye twitching because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The herb's active compounds, including baicalin and wogonoside, have a sedative effect on the body, which can help to relax muscle spasms and reduce tension in the face and eyelids. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, skullcap tea can effectively alleviate eye twitching symptoms, providing natural relief from this common and often annoying condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Skullcap tea's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in skullcap tea have been shown to possess muscle relaxant effects, which can help to calm and soothe twitching muscles in the face, including those around the eyes.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Skullcap tea has a long history of use as an herbal sedative and anxiety-reliever, and its anxiolytic properties may help to reduce stress and tension, which can contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Skullcap plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the muscles around the eyes, relieving eye twitching.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes are underground stems that store nutrients and have been used for centuries to treat nervous disorders, including eye twitching, due to their ability to calm the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried scutellaria baicalensis roots and measure 2-3 teaspoons for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Combine the measured root material with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove any loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before drinking, as hot liquid may exacerbate eye twitching symptoms.
Drink the herbal skullcap tea slowly and comfortably over 15-20 minutes, ideally in the morning or evening to help with relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose teas helps with eye twitching because of its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The omega-6 fatty acids present in evening primrose oil have been shown to soothe irritated tissues and calm muscle spasms, which can help alleviate eye twitching caused by stress, fatigue, or dry eyes. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce redness and itchiness associated with eye irritation. By drinking herbal evening primrose tea regularly, individuals may find relief from persistent and annoying eye twitches.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA is an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dry eyes, which is often a contributing factor to eye twitching.

Antioxidants: Evening primrose tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of twitching.
Calming Effects: The calming effects of evening primrose tea may help to soothe eye muscles and reduce muscle spasms associated with eye twitching, promoting relaxation and reducing tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with eye twitching.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oenothera biennis flowers according to your preference and needs.
Combine one teaspoon of dried evening primrose flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining only the infused liquid.
Serve the evening primrose tea warm or at room temperature within one hour of preparation for maximum efficacy.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye twitching are a natural remedy that combines the soothing properties of herbs to alleviate the annoyance and discomfort caused by eye twitches. By calming the nervous system, these teas help reduce muscle spasms in the eyes, allowing individuals to focus and relax without the constant distraction of twitching eyelids. Examples of herbal teas that effectively combat eye twitching include chamomile, lavender, and passionflower, which have been shown to promote relaxation and calmness. By enjoying a soothing cup of these teas, individuals can reduce the frequency and severity of eye twitching, improving their overall quality of life and reducing stress.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye twitching with herbal teas is by brewing a calming blend of chamomile, lavender, and peppermint. Drink 1-2 cups per day to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye twitching. The soothing properties of these herbs will help relax the muscles around your eyes, alleviating spasms and promoting overall relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye twitching that are treated with herbal teas are various nervous system disorders, such as anxiety, insomnia, and stress-related conditions. Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root have been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of restlessness and jitteriness. Other herbal remedies, like passionflower and lemon balm, may help soothe irritability and promote relaxation. These teas can be consumed hot or cold, depending on personal preference, and are often recommended as a natural and gentle treatment option.
